constraint: POST /checkout p99 = 1.8s; B (notify) and C (analytics) are eligible to move off the request path.
topology_evidence: app/api/checkout/route.ts:42 — sequential awaits on serviceA, serviceB, serviceC
lib/services/notify.ts:88 — no internal queue; direct HTTP
architecture-defaults.md — async transport allowed: in-proc queue OR bus

call_graph: app/api/checkout/route.ts:42 — handleCheckout → serviceA.write — synchronous order write (must stay on-path)
app/api/checkout/route.ts:51 — handleCheckout → notify.send — awaited; offload candidate
app/api/checkout/route.ts:58 — handleCheckout → analytics.record — awaited; offload candidate
lib/services/notify.ts:88 — notify.send → HTTP POST /internal/notify — no internal queue; direct HTTP call
lib/services/analytics.ts:34 — analytics.record → HTTP POST /internal/events — no internal queue; direct HTTP call
defaults_invariants: no-net-new-infra — net-new infrastructure (external bus, managed queue service) requires explicit approval before it appears in a sketch
async-transport-allowed — in-proc queue OR existing bus are both permissible transports for offloading best-effort work
idempotency-required — all async consumer calls must be idempotent; idempotencyKey must be derivable from durable data
flag-gate-required — every behavior change ships behind a feature flag with a documented rollback path

sketches.label: A
sketches.title: In-proc queue (job runner)
sketches.summary: Handler returns after A; B and C enqueue jobs consumed by an in-process worker pool.
sketches.sequence_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant C as Client
  participant H as /checkout
  participant A as ServiceA
  participant Q as JobQueue
  participant W as Worker
  C->>H: POST /checkout
  H->>A: write order (sync)
  A-->>H: ok
  H->>Q: enqueue notify, analytics
  H-->>C: 200 (orderId)
  Q->>W: notify event
  W-->>Q: ack
  Q->>W: analytics event
  alt failure
    W-->>Q: nack
    Q->>Q: retry x3 expo
    Q->>DLQ: dead-letter
  end

sketches.label: B
sketches.title: External message bus (existing)
sketches.summary: Reuse the project's message bus; B and C become consumers in their own deploy units.
sketches.sequence_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant C as Client
  participant H as /checkout
  participant A as ServiceA
  participant B as Bus
  participant N as NotifyConsumer
  participant X as AnalyticsConsumer
  C->>H: POST /checkout
  H->>A: write order (sync)
  A-->>H: ok
  H->>B: publish notify, analytics
  H-->>C: 200
  B->>N: deliver notify
  B->>X: deliver analytics
  alt slow consumer
    B->>B: retry per topic policy
    B->>DLQ: park
  end

recommendation: A — defaults forbid net-new infra without explicit approval; in-proc queue clears the budget and reuses the existing worker pool. B is preferred only if we'll need cross-service consumers within 90 days.
question: A, B, or push back?
